description: SellSnap MVP — Mini store for digital products. Creators upload a file, set a price, share a link, and sell instantly.

userStories.title: Monorepo & project setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a monorepo with shared config so frontend and backend can be developed together.
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm workspace with `apps/web` (React/Vite) and `apps/server` (Fastify) packages
Shared TypeScript config in `packages/tsconfig`
Prisma schema in `packages/db` with PostgreSQL datasource
Root `dev` script starts both web and server concurrently
Web app renders a hello page at http://localhost:5173
Server responds to GET /api/health with { status: 'ok' }
Typecheck passes across all packages
userStories.notes: Use pnpm workspaces. Fastify for server with @fastify/cors configured to allow Vite dev origin. Vite proxy /api to server.

userStories.title: Database schema — User, Product, Purchase tables
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the database schema to support users, products, and purchases.
acceptanceCriteria: User table: id (cuid), name, email (unique), slug (unique), avatarUrl (optional), createdAt

Product table: id (cuid), title, slug (unique), description (text), price (Int, cents), coverImageUrl (optional), filePath, previewContent (optional), published (Boolean, default false), viewCount (Int, default 0), creatorId (FK to User), createdAt, updatedAt

Purchase table: id (cuid), productId (FK to Product), customerEmail, customerName (optional), stripePaymentIntentId, amount (Int, cents), status (String: pending/completed/failed), downloadToken (unique), downloadExpiresAt (DateTime), createdAt
Prisma migration runs successfully against a PostgreSQL database
Prisma client generates without errors
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Schema in packages/db/prisma/schema.prisma. Leave better-auth tables for the auth story — better-auth auto-generates its own tables.

userStories.title: better-auth backend setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I need better-auth configured on the Fastify server with Prisma adapter and email/password enabled.
acceptanceCriteria: Install better-auth and configure with Prisma adapter in apps/server
Email/password authentication enabled
better-auth handler mounted on Fastify (handles /api/auth/* routes)
Auth tables are auto-managed by better-auth (session, account, verification)
BETTER_AUTH_SECRET loaded from .env
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use better-auth's toNodeHandler or Fastify adapter. Mount under /api/auth/*. Create .env.example with BETTER_AUTH_SECRET and DATABASE_URL placeholders.

userStories.title: Auth UI — Sign up and sign in pages
userStories.description: As a creator, I want sign-up and sign-in pages so I can create an account and log in.
acceptanceCriteria: Auth client initialized using createAuthClient from better-auth/react with baseURL pointing to server
Sign-up page at /sign-up with name, email, password fields
Sign-up auto-generates a slug from the user's name (kebab-case)
Sign-in page at /sign-in with email and password fields
After successful sign-up or sign-in, redirect to /dashboard
Form shows error messages on failure
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use Tailwind CSS for styling. Keep forms simple and minimal.

userStories.title: App layout with auth-aware header
userStories.description: As a user, I want a header that shows my name and sign-out button when logged in, or sign-in link when not.
acceptanceCriteria: App layout component wraps all pages with a header
Header shows 'SellSnap' brand/logo linking to /
When authenticated: shows user name and Sign Out button
Sign-out button clears session and redirects to /sign-in
When not authenticated: shows Sign In link
Protected route wrapper that redirects to /sign-in if unauthenticated
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use better-auth's useSession hook for auth state. Apply protected route wrapper to /dashboard/* routes.

userStories.title: Creator dashboard — product list
userStories.description: As a creator, I want a dashboard showing all my products so I can manage them.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard page at /dashboard (protected route)
Fastify route GET /api/products returns all products for the authenticated user
Each product card shows: title, price (formatted as dollars), published status badge, view count, purchase count
Create Product button links to /dashboard/products/new
Empty state message when no products exist
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Purchase count is derived from counting related Purchase records with status 'completed'.

userStories.title: Product creation — backend API with file upload
userStories.description: As a developer, I need API endpoints for creating products with file uploads.
acceptanceCriteria: Install and configure @fastify/multipart for file uploads
Fastify route POST /api/products accepts multipart form data
Fields: title, description, price, previewContent (optional), coverImage (file), productFile (file)
Slug auto-generated from title (kebab-case), ensure uniqueness by appending random suffix if needed
Cover image saved to uploads/images/ directory
Product file saved to uploads/files/ directory
Product created with published: false
Returns created product as JSON
uploads/files/ directory is NOT served by @fastify/static
uploads/images/ directory IS served by @fastify/static at /uploads/images/*
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use @fastify/static to serve uploads/images/ only. Price arrives as dollars string, convert to cents integer.

userStories.title: Product creation — frontend form
userStories.description: As a creator, I want a form to create a new product with file uploads.
acceptanceCriteria: Product creation page at /dashboard/products/new (protected route)
Form fields: title (text), description (textarea), price (number input, in dollars), cover image (file input), digital file (file input), preview content (textarea, optional)
Submits as multipart/form-data to POST /api/products
Shows loading state during submission
On success, redirects to /dashboard/products/:id (edit page)
On error, shows error message
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use FormData for multipart submission. Keep the form simple with Tailwind styling.

userStories.title: Product edit & publish — backend API
userStories.description: As a developer, I need API endpoints for updating and publishing products.
acceptanceCriteria: Fastify route GET /api/products/:id returns product (only if owned by authenticated user)
Fastify route PUT /api/products/:id updates product fields (multipart form data)
Can update: title, description, price, slug, previewContent, coverImage (file), productFile (file)
Slug update validates uniqueness
Fastify route PATCH /api/products/:id/publish toggles the published field
Only the product owner can access these endpoints
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: File fields are optional on update — only replace if a new file is uploaded.

userStories.title: Product edit & publish — frontend form
userStories.description: As a creator, I want to edit my product details and publish/unpublish it.
acceptanceCriteria: Edit page at /dashboard/products/:id (protected, only owner)
Form pre-filled with existing product data fetched from GET /api/products/:id
Can update all fields including slug
Publish/Unpublish toggle button that calls PATCH /api/products/:id/publish
When published, displays the public product URL /p/:slug as a copyable link
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Public product page
userStories.description: As a customer, I want to view a product page so I can decide whether to buy it.
acceptanceCriteria: Fastify route GET /api/products/by-slug/:slug returns product data (public, no auth) and increments viewCount
Frontend page at /p/:slug (public, no auth required)
Displays: product title, description, preview content (if any), cover image, price, creator name
Buy Now button visible (functionality added in checkout story)
Shows 404 page if product doesn't exist or is unpublished
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: viewCount incremented server-side in the API route, not on the client.

userStories.title: Creator public profile page
userStories.description: As a customer, I want to see all products by a creator on their profile page.
acceptanceCriteria: Fastify route GET /api/creators/:slug returns creator info and their published products
Frontend page at /creator/:creatorSlug (public)
Shows creator name, avatar (if set), and list of published products
Each product card links to /p/:productSlug
Shows 404 if creator slug doesn't exist
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use /creator/:slug frontend route to avoid conflicts with other top-level routes.

userStories.title: Stripe checkout integration
userStories.description: As a customer, I want to pay for a product via Stripe so I can download it.
acceptanceCriteria: Install stripe Node.js SDK
Fastify route POST /api/checkout/:productSlug creates a Stripe Checkout Session
Checkout session includes product title, price, metadata (productId)
Accepts customerEmail in request body and passes to Checkout Session
Returns checkout session URL; frontend redirects to it
On success, Stripe redirects to /purchase/success?session_id={id}
On cancel, Stripe redirects back to /p/:slug
Buy Now button on product page calls this endpoint and redirects
STRIPE_SECRET_KEY loaded from .env
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use Stripe Checkout in payment mode (one-time). FRONTEND_URL env var for redirect URLs.

userStories.title: Stripe webhook — payment confirmation
userStories.description: As the system, I need to process Stripe webhooks to confirm payments and create purchase records.
acceptanceCriteria: Fastify route POST /api/webhooks/stripe handles checkout.session.completed event
Raw body access configured for webhook signature verification
Verifies webhook signature using STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET
Creates Purchase record with status 'completed', stripePaymentIntentId from session
Generates unique downloadToken (UUID) and downloadExpiresAt (24 hours from now)
STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET loaded from .env
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use Fastify's addContentTypeParser for raw body on the webhook route. Extract customerEmail and productId from session metadata.

userStories.title: Download page and secure file delivery
userStories.description: As a customer, after payment I want to download my purchased file immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: Fastify route GET /api/purchases/by-session/:sessionId returns purchase details (product title, downloadToken)
Frontend success page at /purchase/success fetches purchase using session_id query param
Displays 'Thank you' message with product title and Download button
Fastify route GET /api/download/:token validates token and checks downloadExpiresAt
Rate limiting on download endpoint using @fastify/rate-limit
Returns file with Content-Disposition: attachment header
Returns 404 if token invalid, 410 if expired
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Download token is single-use concept — but for MVP just check expiration. Rate limit to ~10 requests per minute per IP.

userStories.title: Purchase confirmation email
userStories.description: As a customer, I want to receive an email with my download link after purchase.
acceptanceCriteria: After purchase is confirmed in webhook handler, send email to customerEmail
Email contains: product title, download link (/api/download/:token), expiration notice (24h)
In development, log email content to console instead of sending
Email service abstracted behind a simple sendEmail function
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Console.log is acceptable for dev. Structure the sendEmail function so it can be swapped for Resend or similar later.

userStories.title: Creator sales analytics
userStories.description: As a creator, I want to see basic sales metrics for my products.
acceptanceCriteria: Fastify route GET /api/analytics returns per-product stats for authenticated creator
Stats include: product title, view count, purchase count, total revenue (sum of completed purchase amounts)
Also returns aggregate totals: total views, total purchases, total revenue
Analytics section displayed on /dashboard page or /dashboard/analytics
Revenue displayed formatted as dollars
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use Prisma groupBy or aggregate queries. Purchase count = count of purchases with status 'completed'.

userStories.title: Creator profile settings
userStories.description: As a creator, I want to update my profile (name, slug, avatar).
acceptanceCriteria: Settings page at /dashboard/settings (protected)
Fastify route PUT /api/profile accepts multipart form with name, slug, avatar (file)
Slug uniqueness validated; shows error if taken
Avatar image saved to uploads/images/ directory
Can update display name, slug, avatar
Success message shown after update
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Slug change affects the public creator profile URL.
